title: Marangu Route
description: Known as the 'Coca-Cola Route', Marangu is the most popular and affordable route on Kilimanjaro. It features permanent sleeping huts at each campsite and is the only route where ascent and descent share the same path. The shorter duration means less acclimatization time, resulting in a lower summit success rate.
highlights: Only route with permanent sleeping huts
Shortest and most affordable route
Passes through rainforest, heathland, moorland, and alpine desert
Summit night via Gillman's Point to Uhuru Peak

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to hotel in Moshi. Evening briefing with mountain guide.

itinerary.title: Marangu Gate – Mandara Huts
itinerary.description: Trek begins through rainforest to Mandara Hut. Trail can be muddy and slippery at lower elevations.

itinerary.title: Mandara Huts – Horombo Huts
itinerary.description: Emerge from rainforest into heathland and open moorlands. Stop halfway for lunch with views of Mawenzi.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Kibo Huts
itinerary.description: Trek through dwindling heathland into a moonscape across the saddle connecting Mawenzi and Kibo. Rest and hydrate in preparation for summit night.

itinerary.title: Kibo Huts – Uhuru Peak – Horombo Huts
itinerary.description: Early start between midnight and 2am. Ascend via switchbacks through heavy scree to Gillman's Point on the crater rim, then on to Uhuru Peak. Descend back to Horombo Huts via Kibo.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Marangu Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descend through Mandara Huts for a rest stop, continuing to Marangu Gate where summit certificates are presented. Transfer back to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

excluded: Private portable toilet (available at extra cost)

title: Marangu Route
description: Known as the 'Coca-Cola Route', the 6-day Marangu offers an extra acclimatization day at Horombo Huts compared to the 5-day version, improving summit success rates. Features permanent sleeping huts throughout and is the only route where ascent and descent share the same path.
highlights: Extra acclimatization day at Horombo Huts
Only route with permanent sleeping huts
Passes Gillman's Point to Uhuru Peak on summit night
Less physical demand due to gradual ascent profile

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer and overnight in Moshi. Briefing and gear check with head guide.

itinerary.title: Marangu Gate – Mandara Huts
itinerary.description: 45-minute drive to Marangu Gate. Trek through rainforest on muddy, slippery trails to Mandara Huts.

itinerary.title: Mandara Huts – Horombo Huts
itinerary.description: Ascend through heathland and open moorlands with views of Mawenzi. Arrive at Horombo Huts late afternoon.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Acclimatization Day
itinerary.description: Rest day at Horombo Huts. Optional stroll to Mawenzi base camp and back. Extra day aids acclimatization before the push to Kibo.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Kibo Huts
itinerary.description: Trek through the saddle connecting Mawenzi and Kibo across semi-desert moonscape. Rest and prepare gear for summit night.

itinerary.title: Kibo Huts – Uhuru Peak – Horombo Huts
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Switchback ascent through scree to Gillman's Point then on to Uhuru Peak. Descend to Horombo Huts via Kibo for lunch.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Marangu Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Final descent through Mandara Huts. Summit certificates presented at Marangu Gate. Transfer back to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

excluded: Beverages (water, soda and alcohol at hotels)

title: Umbwe Route
description: The steepest and most direct route to the Southern Glaciers, Western Breach, and Uhuru Peak. Widely considered the most challenging non-technical route on Kilimanjaro. Best suited to experienced trekkers who prefer solitude and a fast ascent. Very low traffic makes it one of the most wilderness-like experiences on the mountain.
highlights: Steepest and most challenging route
Extremely low foot traffic
Most direct path to Southern Glaciers and Western Breach
Dense montane forest ridge between two deep valleys

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Umbwe Gate – Umbwe Cave
itinerary.description: Drive to Kilimanjaro National Park Gate. Trek through dense montane rainforest along a ridge between two deep valleys. Trail is muddy and slippery — gaiters and trekking poles highly recommended.

itinerary.title: Umbwe Cave – Barranco Camp
itinerary.description: Steep ascent along the Umbwe ridge with a slight descent into Barranco Camp in the valley. Temperatures begin to drop.

itinerary.title: Barranco Camp – Karanga Camp – Barafu Camp
itinerary.description: Steep ascent up the Barranco Wall to Karanga Valley, then continue up to Barafu Camp completing the South Circuit with panoramic summit views. Early dinner and rest before summit night.

itinerary.title: Barafu Camp – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Ascent between Rebmann and Ratzel glaciers via switchbacks to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend steeply to Mweka Camp via Barafu for lunch.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Final descent to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer back to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

title: Umbwe Route
description: The 6-day Umbwe offers one extra day over the 5-day version, splitting the Barranco–Barafu section across two days for slightly better acclimatization. Still the steepest and most direct route on Kilimanjaro, best suited to experienced trekkers seeking low traffic and raw wilderness.
highlights: Steepest and most challenging route
Extra day for improved acclimatization vs 5-day
Very low foot traffic
Barranco Wall ascent — one of Kilimanjaro's most dramatic sections

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Umbwe Gate – Umbwe Cave
itinerary.description: Drive to Kilimanjaro National Park Gate. Trek through dense montane rainforest along a ridge between two deep valleys.

itinerary.title: Umbwe Cave – Barranco Camp
itinerary.description: Steep ascent along the Umbwe ridge with a slight descent into Barranco Camp in the valley.

itinerary.title: Barranco Camp – Karanga Camp
itinerary.description: Steep ascent up the adventurous Barranco Wall to Karanga Valley. One of the most dramatic days on any Kilimanjaro route.

itinerary.title: Karanga Camp – Barafu Camp
itinerary.description: Continue up to Barafu Camp completing the South Circuit. Panoramic views of the summit from multiple angles. Early dinner and rest before summit night.

itinerary.title: Barafu Camp – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Ascent via switchbacks between glaciers to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend steeply to Mweka Camp.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Final descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer back to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request

title: Machame Route
description: Known as the 'Whisky Route', Machame is one of the most scenic and popular routes on Kilimanjaro. It passes through magnificent forests, moorland zones, the Shira Plateau, and the dramatic Barranco Wall before reaching the summit via Barafu. A more manageable incline and longer profile give it a higher summit success rate than shorter routes.
highlights: One of the most scenic routes on Kilimanjaro
Passes the dramatic Barranco Wall
Crosses the Shira Plateau
Higher summit success rate due to better acclimatization profile

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Machame Gate – Machame Camp
itinerary.description: 45-minute drive to Machame Gate through the Village of Machame. Trek through rainforest on trails that can be muddy and slippery.

itinerary.title: Machame Camp – Shira Camp
itinerary.description: Emerge from rainforest, cross the valley along a steep rocky ridge, then west along a river gorge to Shira campsite.

itinerary.title: Shira Camp – Lava Tower – Barranco Camp
itinerary.description: Important acclimatization day. Trek east to Lava Tower (Shark's Tooth, 4,650m) before descending to Barranco Camp — classic climb high, sleep low.

itinerary.title: Barranco Camp – Karanga Camp – Barafu Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend the Barranco Wall to Karanga Valley, then continue to Barafu Camp completing the South Circuit. Early dinner before summit night.

itinerary.title: Barafu Camp – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Ascent via switchbacks between glaciers to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend steeply to Mweka Camp.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages

Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate

excluded: Beverages (water, soda and alcohol at hotels)

title: Machame Route
description: The 7-day Machame — the 'Whisky Route' — splits the Barranco–Barafu section across two days, providing superior acclimatization and the highest success rates on this route. Widely regarded as one of the best Kilimanjaro treks for combining scenery, challenge, and realistic summit chances.
highlights: Best acclimatization profile of the Machame options
Separate Barranco and Karanga camp days
Crosses Shira Plateau and passes Lava Tower
Summit via Stella Point and Uhuru Peak

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Machame Gate – Machame Camp
itinerary.description: 45-minute drive to Machame Gate. Trek through rainforest on muddy trails.

itinerary.title: Machame Camp – Shira Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend out of the rainforest across the valley along a steep rocky ridge, turning west along a river gorge to Shira.

itinerary.title: Shira Camp – Lava Tower – Barranco Camp
itinerary.description: Key acclimatization day. Trek to Lava Tower (Shark's Tooth, 4,650m) then descend to Barranco Camp.

itinerary.title: Barranco Camp – Karanga Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend the Barranco Wall to Karanga Valley. Overnight at Karanga for additional acclimatization.

itinerary.title: Karanga Camp – Barafu Camp
itinerary.description: Short ascent to Barafu Camp, completing the South Circuit. Rest and prepare for summit night.

itinerary.title: Barafu Camp – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Switchback ascent between glaciers to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Steep descent to Mweka Camp.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request

title: Rongai Route
description: The only route approaching Kilimanjaro from the north, near the Kenyan border. Remote and less crowded, Rongai offers a unique wilderness experience with possible sightings of antelope, elephant, and buffalo. Descent is via the Marangu Route. Its flatter profile means less natural acclimatization, so the 7-day version is often recommended.
highlights: Only route approaching from the north
Low traffic with true wilderness feel
Wildlife sightings possible (antelope, elephant, buffalo)
Descent via Marangu Route

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Nale Muru Gate – Simba Camp
itinerary.description: 4–5 hour drive through coffee plantations and Nale Muru village to the park gate. Trek through wooded scenery to Simba Camp near the moorland zone edge, with views of Kenya's plains.

itinerary.title: Simba Camp – Second Cave Camp
itinerary.description: Consistent ascent through moorland with views of Kibo and first glimpse of the Eastern crater ice fields.

itinerary.title: Second Cave Camp – Third Cave Camp
itinerary.description: Enter semi-desert terrain. Shorter day bringing you closer to the Eastern ice fields.

itinerary.title: Third Cave Camp – Kibo Huts
itinerary.description: Enter Alpine Desert terrain and trek to Kibo Huts just below the crater wall. Merge with the Marangu Route. Prepare gear and rest early before summit attempt.

itinerary.title: Kibo Huts – Uhuru Peak – Horombo Huts
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Ascent via switchbacks through scree to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend to Horombo Huts via Kibo for lunch.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Marangu Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descend via Marangu Route, stopping at Mandara Huts for lunch. Summit certificates at Marangu Gate. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

excluded: Beverages (water, soda and alcohol at hotels)

title: Rongai Route
description: The 7-day Rongai adds Kikelewa Camp and Mawenzi Tarn to the itinerary, providing significantly better acclimatization on this northern approach. Mawenzi Tarn sits in a spectacular cirque beneath the towering spires of Mawenzi and offers optional acclimatization hikes. Descent is via the Marangu Route.
highlights: Spectacular Mawenzi Tarn camp beneath Mawenzi spires
Best acclimatization of the Rongai options
Wildlife sightings possible on northern approach
Lunar Saddle crossing between Mawenzi and Kibo

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Nale Muru Gate – Simba Camp
itinerary.description: 4–5 hour drive through coffee plantations. Trek through wooded scenery to Simba Camp with views of Kenya's plains.

itinerary.title: Simba Camp – Second Cave Camp
itinerary.description: Consistent ascent through moorland with views of Kibo and the Eastern crater ice fields.

itinerary.title: Second Cave Camp – Kikelewa Camp
itinerary.description: Steady ascent across moorland towards the jagged peaks of Mawenzi.

itinerary.title: Kikelewa Camp – Mawenzi Tarn
itinerary.description: Short but steep climb to Mawenzi Tarn, spectacularly situated in a cirque beneath the spires of Mawenzi. Afternoon free to rest or explore for acclimatization.

itinerary.title: Mawenzi Tarn – Kibo Hut
itinerary.description: Cross the lunar desert Saddle between Mawenzi and Kibo. Rest at Kibo Hut in preparation for summit night.

itinerary.title: Kibo Huts – Uhuru Peak – Horombo Huts
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Ascent via switchbacks through scree to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend to Horombo Huts via Kibo for lunch.

itinerary.title: Horombo Huts – Marangu Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descent via Marangu Route, stopping at Mandara Huts for lunch. Summit certificates at Marangu Gate. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request

Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate

title: Lemosho Route
description: A newer, more remote and scenic route starting from the western side of Kilimanjaro at Londorossi Gate. Passes through beautiful forests and moorlands before crossing the Shira Plateau and joining the Machame Route at Lava Tower. Wildlife including buffalo and elephant may be spotted in the early stages. Longer duration allows excellent acclimatization and high summit success rates.
highlights: Remote western approach with possible wildlife sightings
Crosses the Shira Plateau — one of Earth's highest plateaus
Passes Lava Tower (Shark's Tooth) at 4,650m
High summit success rate due to gradual ascent

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Lemosho Gate – Mti Mkubwa Camp
itinerary.description: 45-minute drive to Kilimanjaro National Park Gate. Trek through rainforest to Mti Mkubwa (Big Tree) Camp.

itinerary.title: Mti Mkubwa Camp – Shira 2 Camp
itinerary.description: Leave the forest, climbing steadily with wide views to the rim of the Shira Plateau and on to Shira 2 Camp.

itinerary.title: Shira 2 Camp – Lava Tower – Barranco Camp
itinerary.description: Key acclimatization day. Trek east to Lava Tower (4,650m) then descend to Barranco Camp — classic climb high, sleep low.

itinerary.title: Barranco Camp – Karanga Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend the Barranco Wall to Karanga Valley. Overnight at Karanga for acclimatization.

itinerary.title: Karanga Camp – Barafu Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend to Barafu Camp completing the South Circuit. Panoramic summit views. Rest before summit night.

itinerary.title: Barafu Camp – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Switchback ascent between glaciers to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Steep descent to Mweka Camp.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

title: Lemosho Route
description: The 8-day Lemosho adds a full Shira Plateau exploration day and a separate Shira 1 to Shira 2 stage, making it the most thorough acclimatization version of this already excellent route. Starts on Kilimanjaro's western side, passes through rainforest, moorland, and the Shira Plateau before joining the Southern Circuit to Barafu and the summit.
highlights: Full day exploring the Shira Plateau — one of Earth's highest
Best acclimatization of the Lemosho options
Wildlife possible in early rainforest stages
Separate Shira 1 and Shira 2 camp stages

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Lemosho Gate – Mti Mkubwa Camp
itinerary.description: Drive to park gate. Trek through rainforest to Mti Mkubwa (Big Tree) Camp.

itinerary.title: Mti Mkubwa Camp – Shira 1 Camp
itinerary.description: Leave the rainforest. Steady climb with wide views to the rim of the Shira Plateau and on to Shira 1 Camp.

itinerary.title: Shira 1 Camp – Shira 2 Camp
itinerary.description: Explore the Shira Plateau — one of the highest plateaus on Earth. Gentle walk east across moorland meadows to Shira 2 Camp.

itinerary.title: Shira 2 Camp – Lava Tower – Barranco Camp
itinerary.description: Key acclimatization day. Trek east to Lava Tower (Shark's Tooth, 4,650m) then descend to Barranco Camp.

itinerary.title: Barranco Camp – Karanga Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend the Barranco Wall to Karanga Valley.

itinerary.title: Karanga Camp – Barafu Camp
itinerary.description: Ascend to Barafu Camp completing the South Circuit. Rest and prepare for summit night.

itinerary.title: Barafu Camp – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Switchback ascent between glaciers to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Steep descent to Mweka Camp.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Final descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

title: Northern Circuit
description: The longest route on Kilimanjaro and one of the best, offering nearly 360 degrees of scenery including the rarely visited northern slopes. The route approaches from the west at Lemosho Gate, crosses the Shira Plateau, then circles clockwise around the north of Kibo via Moir Hut and Buffalo Camp before summiting from the east via School Hut. Highest overall summit success rate of any route.
highlights: Nearly 360-degree scenic circuit around Kilimanjaro
Rarely visited northern slopes — very low traffic
Highest summit success rate on the mountain
Summit from the east via School Hut — unique perspective

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Lemosho Gate – Mti Mkubwa Camp
itinerary.description: Drive approximately 2 hours to Londorossi Gate. Trek through rainforest to Mti Mkubwa Camp. Wildlife possible including elephant and buffalo.

itinerary.title: Mti Mkubwa Camp – Shira 1 Camp
itinerary.description: Through the last rainforest section into low alpine moorland, climbing to Shira Plateau and Shira 1 Camp.

itinerary.title: Shira 1 – Moir Hut
itinerary.description: Long trek east through the Garden of the Seneca into high alpine desert. Reach Lava Tower (Shark's Tooth, 4,600m) for lunch then descend to Moir Camp — classic climb high, sleep low acclimatization.

itinerary.title: Moir Camp – Buffalo Camp
itinerary.description: Moderately steep climb out of Moir Valley. Optional detour to Little Lent Hill summit (4,375m). Route skirts the northern Kibo slopes with spectacular views toward the Kenya/Tanzania border plains.

itinerary.title: Buffalo Camp – Rongai Third Cave
itinerary.description: Climb Buffalo ridge then down into Pofu Camp for lunch. Continue east around the northern slopes to Rongai Third Cave.

itinerary.title: Rongai Third Cave – School Hut
itinerary.description: Steady ascent over the Saddle between Kibo and Mawenzi then southwest to School Hut. Early dinner and rest before midnight summit start.

itinerary.title: School Hut – Uhuru Peak – Millennium Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Switchback ascent via scree to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend to Millennium Camp via Barafu for lunch.

itinerary.title: Millennium Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)

title: Northern Circuit
description: The most comprehensive Kilimanjaro experience available. The 9-day Northern Circuit adds a full Shira 2 stage and separate Moir camp day, providing the best possible acclimatization on the mountain. Circles nearly 360 degrees around Kilimanjaro including the remote northern slopes, before a final push to Uhuru Peak via School Hut. The highest recommended summit success rate of any route.
highlights: Most comprehensive circuit of Kilimanjaro available
Best acclimatization profile of all routes
Full Shira Plateau crossing with Shira 1 and Shira 2 camps
Rarely visited northern slopes

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.description: Arrival at Kilimanjaro International Airport, transfer to Moshi. Briefing, gear check and equipment hire arrangements.

itinerary.title: Lemosho Gate – Mti Mkubwa Camp
itinerary.description: 45-minute drive to park gate through mountainside villages. Trek through rainforest to Mti Mkubwa Camp.

itinerary.title: Mti Mkubwa Camp – Shira 1 Camp
itinerary.description: Leave the rainforest and climb steadily with wide views to the Shira Plateau rim and on to Shira 1 Camp.

itinerary.title: Shira 1 Camp – Shira 2 Camp
itinerary.description: Cross the Shira Plateau — one of Earth's highest — from Shira 1 to Shira 2 Camp on moorland meadows.

itinerary.title: Shira 2 Camp – Lava Tower – Moir Hut
itinerary.description: Trek east to Lava Tower (Shark's Tooth, 4,650m) for acclimatization then join the Northern Circuit descending to Moir Hut.

itinerary.title: Moir Camp – Buffalo Camp
itinerary.description: Moderately steep climb out of Moir Valley with optional detour to Little Lent Hill. Route skirts northern Kibo slopes with panoramic views to Kenya.

itinerary.title: Buffalo Camp – Rongai Third Cave
itinerary.description: Climb Buffalo ridge, down to Pofu Camp for lunch, continue east around the northern slopes to Rongai Third Cave.

itinerary.title: Third Cave – School Hut
itinerary.description: Steady ascent over the Saddle between Kibo and Mawenzi, then southwest to School Hut. Early dinner and rest before midnight summit start.

itinerary.title: School Hut – Uhuru Peak – Mweka Camp
itinerary.description: Midnight start. Switchback ascent via scree to Stella Point then Uhuru Peak. Descend steeply to Mweka Camp via Barafu for lunch.

itinerary.title: Mweka Camp – Mweka Gate – Moshi
itinerary.description: Descent through rainforest to Mweka Gate. Summit certificates presented. Transfer to Moshi.

itinerary.title: Departure
itinerary.description: Transfer to Kilimanjaro International Airport.

crew: English speaking and Wilderness First Responder trained
equipment: Wash basins with hot water morning and evening
food_and_drink: Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free on request
fees: Kilimanjaro National Park fees and VAT
Camping/hut, rescue, conservation fees and taxes
Fair crew wages
Pre-trek briefing
accommodation_and_transfers: Round-trip airport transfer (JRO–Moshi)
Round-trip transfer Moshi to park gate
2 hotel nights in Moshi (bed and breakfast)
